en: As for the one who is weak in faith, welcome him, but not to quarrel over opinions.
zh: 你們要接納信心軟弱的人，不要論斷引起爭論的事。

en: One person believes he may eat anything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
zh: 有人相信所有的食物都可以吃，信心軟弱的人卻只吃蔬菜。

en: Let not the one who eats despise the one who abstains, and let not the one who abstains pass judgment on the one who eats, for God has welcomed him.
zh: 吃的人不要輕看不吃的人，不吃的人也不要批評吃的人，因為 神已經接納他了。

en: Who are you to pass judgment on the servant of another? It is before his own master that he stands or falls. And he will be upheld, for the Lord is able to make him stand.
zh: 你是誰，竟然批評別人的家僕呢？他或站穩或跌倒，只和自己的主人有關；但他必定站穩，因為主能夠使他站穩。

en: One person esteems one day as better than another, while another esteems all days alike. Each one should be fully convinced in his own mind.
zh: 有人認為這日比那日好，也有人認為日日都是一樣；只要各人自己心意堅定就可以了。

en: The one who observes the day, observes it in honor of the Lord. The one who eats, eats in honor of the Lord, since he gives thanks to God, while the one who abstains, abstains in honor of the Lord and gives thanks to God.
zh: 守日的人是為主守的，吃的人是為主吃的，因為他感謝 神；不吃的人是為主不吃，他也感謝 神。

en: For none of us lives to himself, and none of us dies to himself.
zh: 我們沒有一個人為自己活，也沒有一個人為自己死。我們若活著，是為主而活；

en: If we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So then, whether we live or whether we die, we are the Lord's.
zh: 我們若死了，是為主而死。所以，我們無論活著或是死了，總是屬於主的人。

en: For to this end Christ died and lived again, that he might be Lord both of the dead and of the living.
zh: 為了這緣故，基督死了，又活過來，就是要作死人和活人的主。

en: Why do you pass judgment on your brother? Or you, why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God;
zh: 這樣，你為甚麼批評你的弟兄呢？為甚麼又輕看你的弟兄呢？我們都要站在 神的審判臺前；

en: for it is written, "As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God."
zh: 因為經上記著： “主說，我指著我的永生起誓： 萬膝必向我跪拜， 萬口必稱頌 神。”

en: So then each of us will give an account of himself to God.
zh: 這樣看來，我們各人都要把自己的事向 神交代。

en: Therefore let us not pass judgment on one another any longer, but rather decide never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother.
zh: 所以，我們不要再彼此批評了；倒要立定主意，決不作絆倒弟兄或使他跌倒的事。

en: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself, but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean.
zh: 我知道，並且在主耶穌裡深信，沒有一樣東西的本身是不潔的，但如果有人認為是不潔的，對他來說那東西就成為不潔了。

en: For if your brother is grieved by what you eat, you are no longer walking in love. By what you eat, do not destroy the one for whom Christ died.
zh: 如果你為了食物使你的弟兄憂愁，你就不再是憑著愛心行事了。你不可因著你的食物，使基督已經替他死了的人滅亡。

en: So do not let what you regard as good be spoken of as evil.
zh: 所以，不可使你們看為好的被人毀謗；

en: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking but of righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
zh: 因為 神的國不在於吃喝，而在於公義、和睦，以及聖靈裡的喜樂。

en: Whoever thus serves Christ is acceptable to God and approved by men.
zh: 這樣服事基督的人，必蒙 神喜悅，又得眾人嘉許。

en: So then let us pursue what makes for peace and for mutual upbuilding.
zh: 所以，我們總要追求和睦的事，與彼此造就的事。

en: Do not, for the sake of food, destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for anyone to make another stumble by what he eats.
zh: 不可因食物的緣故拆毀 神的工作。一切都是潔淨的，但人若因食物絆倒弟兄，對他來說，這就是惡事了。

en: It is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that causes your brother to stumble.
zh: 無論是吃肉，是喝酒，或是甚麼使你的弟兄跌倒的事，一律不要作才好。

en: The faith that you have, keep between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who has no reason to pass judgment on himself for what he approves.
zh: 你所信的，你自己要在 神面前持守。人在經自己考驗後認可的事上能夠不自責，他就有福了。

en: But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats, because the eating is not from faith. For whatever does not proceed from faith is sin.
zh: 但如果有人存著疑惑的心去吃，他就被定罪了，因為他不是出於信心。凡不是出於信心的，都是罪。
